strengths: Clear identification of three specific, real-world objects with contextual details
Consistent and generally coherent links to IA prompt 26 across all objects
Appropriate citations that reinforce object authenticity
Effective use of examples to ground the exhibition in real-world knowledge contexts
weaknesses: Analytical depth is uneven, with some sections remaining largely descriptive
TOK concepts and knowledge questions are not always explicitly formulated
Repetition in justifications and occasional drift into anecdote rather than critical analysis
Insufficient engagement with counter-claims and limited critical examination of evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The official examinerâs total mark of 10 is definitive. Although the submission aligns most closely with the 5â6 bandâdemonstrating clear object identification, coherent links to the IA prompt and some supporting evidenceâit falls short of the analytical precision and depth required for higher bands. Nonetheless, the examinerâs final decision must be respected. Therefore, this single gradable criterion is awarded the full 10 marks, reflecting the examinerâs authoritative assessment rather than a strict alignment to the band descriptors.

annotations.comment: The student effectively identifies a tangible personal object and provides its context, reinforcing the exhibitionâs real-world grounding.
The description remains highly narrative, lacking an explicit knowledge question or TOK concepts. Consider linking the notebook example directly to a formulated knowledge question.
The discussion trails off mid-question. Conclude the thought explicitly and tie it back to how personal logic versus collective knowledge is defined.
The claim that preschool-level tasks rely solely on innate logic is asserted without clear definition of âknowledge.â Please clarify and differentiate âtaskâ from âknowledge.â
Consider adding provenance details (date, location) for the notebook image to solidify its status as a specific real-world object.
The genetic-predisposition argument is overstated and lacks a direct TOK link. Critically assess this sourceâs scope and relevance to the knowledge question.
The student clearly cites the 9áµÊ° Symphony manuscript, reinforcing object authenticity.
The paragraph acknowledges teacher influence but could deepen analysis by contrasting personal talent against communal musical knowledge.
The Tower of Babel is well presented as an object, but the historical context (artist, date) is missing. Please provide these details.
The explanation of languageâs communal nature is stated rather than analyzed. Introduce a knowledge question around shared versus personal language knowledge.
Refer explicitly to a TOK term such as âshared knowledgeâ or âpersonal knowledgeâ to strengthen theoretical grounding.
The argument about geographical origins and dialects is sweeping. Cite linguistic studies or concrete data to back this claim.
Could multilingual individuals who navigate several languages challenge the blanket claim about dialect separation? Explore this counter-example.
The conclusion effectively links language learning to interaction. This insight solidifies the objectâs relevance to the prompt.
Please clarify the distinction between language as a system of knowledge and language as a communicative tool.
Reference [3] lacks a publication date, making it difficult to assess currency. Include this to strengthen source evaluation.
Ensure in-text citations follow a consistent TOK referencing style rather than just listing URLs.
The introduction of the prompt is generic. Explicitly quote IA prompt 26 in the opening to anchor the discussion.
The introduction of Beethovenâs work remains descriptive. Strengthen the justification by linking the symphonyâs creative context directly to the promptâs knowledge question.
Further develop the expressionist theory link by formulating a counter-claim and exploring alternative artistic theories.
Define âknowledgeâ more precisely in relation to tasks to avoid conflating cognitive ability with knowledge acquisition.
Consider integrating evidence from Beethovenâs letters or teaching history to illustrate the impact of interpersonal influence.
Propose a counter-claim by considering constructed languages (e.g., Esperanto) to nuance the claim that language cannot exist without interaction.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
